text:
Team GBathlete Holly Bradshaw was in ‘floods of tears’ after quite literally crashing out of theParis2024Olympics.

Bradshaw, competing at her final Olympic Games, failed to clear 4.40m after three attempts, bowing out of the women’s pole vault at the Stade de France.

The 32-year-old cleared 4.85m to claim bronze at Tokyo 2020 but could not get anywhere near that height in Paris.

One of her attempts went badly wrong and she was sent flying by the pole, almost crashing into a cameraman positioned on the side of the runway.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

When another camera picked Bradshaw up she was getting to her feet from the grass inside the Olympic running track and brushing herself down.

Bradshaw initially looked relatively calm but then burst into tears as the reality of struggling at her final Olympics sunk in.

She slumped to her knees beside the pole vault runway and was consoled by fellow Team GB pole vaulter Molly Caudery, who entered the competition at 4.55m.

Molly Caudery consoles Holly Bradshaw after the Team GB pole vaulter’s Olympics came to an end in qualifying. â¤ï¸#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/no30iB9IuE

‘Molly should qualify, as at the moment she’s the best in the world,’ former GB 400m runner Katharine Merry said on BBC Radio 5 Live.

‘It’s a real passing of the pole in British pole vaulting, as the Olympic bronze medallist Holly Bradshaw has sadly left the competition with three failures – it’s a real changing of the guard.

‘Bradshaw is in floods of tears having being eliminated, she is being consoled by Caudery.’

Unfortunately there was more pole vault misery for Team GB as Caudery, 24, also crashed out of the event after failing to make 4.55m at three attempts.

Caudery came into the Games having won the world indoor title and setting a British record earlier this year but failed to reproduce that form in Paris.

On Caudery’s exit, Merry added: ‘Molly Caudery has exited the Olympic Games. She has had three failures at her opening height and she took the bar down.

“It’s totally heartbreaking.” ðAn emotional Molly Caudery reacts to her early exit from the women’s pole vault ð©#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/2BPKWml0OW

‘She is thanking the crowd, but her head is in her hands – one of the big medal hopes is out.

‘Nobody is going to believe this. This is the biggest shock in the track and field of this Olympics.

‘Molly Caudery has gone over a height of 4.92m [this season] – 4cm higher than anyone else in the world.

‘She decided to come in at a opening height of 4.55m. In her pool nobody else entered at that height, everyone else came in at an earlier height.’

Following Caudery’s shock exit, it was Bradshaw’s turn to offer a shoulder to cry on as Great Britain’s hopes in the women’s pole vault were quickly shattered.

An emotional Caudery then toldBBC Sport: ‘It’s totally heartbreaking. This is not the Olympic experience I was hoping for.

‘I’m just so sorry to everyone back home, I wish I could have done better but I’m going to try and learn from this. It’s still an Olympic experience and I’ll take everything I can from it.

‘I felt great and I’m in the best shape of my life. I didn’t feel too nervous – I love a big crowd – but for some reason it wasn’t my day.’

title: Team GB athlete bursts into tears after terrible Olympic pole vault attempt | Metro News
description: Team GB athlete Holly Bradshaw was in 'floods of tears' after quite literally crashing out of the Paris 2024 Olympics.
